title: HeroQuest - Episode 1 - My history with HQ / a look at the base games
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qd6gmV0qrrk

Daniel launches a three-part HeroQuest series — and starts with an admission: he doesn't have the nostalgia. Unlike most HeroQuest devotees who imprinted on the game in childhood, Daniel comes to it with fresh eyes, comparing the 1989 Milton Bradley original against the 2022 Hasbro/Avalon Hill reimagining.

This episode is part personal history, part comparative review. Both base games get examined side by side — components, quest design, solo viability, and whether the new edition captures what made the original a gateway drug for an entire generation of dungeon crawlers. At 40+ minutes, it's thorough without overstaying its welcome.

----

Did you play HeroQuest as a kid, or did you come to it later? Does nostalgia change how you see it?
